title: /kernel
summary_text: - `Kernel` is a single-sovereign, Codex-first run model: `1 request = 1 Kernel run = 1 tmux session = 1 Codex thread` (`requirements-freeze-v1`, `schema-v1`). - Minimum council shape for `requirements/plan/critique` is `Codex latest + GLM + specialist x1`; `Claude` is optional, not a minimum requirement (`contracts.md`, `requirements-freeze-v1.md`). - `Simulation` gate minimum is `codex-spark only`; fallback to `codex-subagent` requires explicit human approval (`contracts.md`, `requirements-freeze-v1.md`). - `Implementation/verification` minimum is `Codex latest + codex-subagent + GLM`; for design/UI/UX work, `gemini-cli` is additionally required (`contracts.md`, `requirements-freeze-v1.md`). - Execution must pass mandatory gates in order (`requirements -> plan -> simulation -> critique -> replan -> execution`), and required phase artifacts must exist or completion fails closed (`contracts.md`, `requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md`). - Runtime truth for a plain `/kernel` run is artifact/state-driven: bootstrap receipt + runtime ledger + compact artifact (doctor is read-only), with lifecycle/state modeled as healthy/degraded/blocked and Mac mini as primary node with MBP continuation (`schema-v1.md`, `requirements-freeze-v1.md`).
